note_data.title: Pharmacology
keyPoints: Pharmacology is the branch of biology that deals with the study of drugs, their sources, properties, effects on living organisms and their therapeutic uses
Drugs are chemical substances that affect the structure and function of living organisms when introduced into the body
Drug sources include plants (digitalis from foxglove), animals (insulin from pancreas), minerals (iron supplements), and synthetic sources
Drug classification includes stimulants (increase nervous system activity), depressants (decrease nervous system activity), hallucinogens (alter perception), and therapeutic drugs
Antibiotics are substances produced by microorganisms that kill or inhibit the growth of other microorganisms — discovered by Alexander Fleming
Drug abuse refers to the excessive and harmful use of drugs that leads to physical and psychological dependence
Tolerance occurs when the body requires increasingly larger doses of a drug to achieve the same effect
Withdrawal symptoms appear when a drug-dependent person stops taking the drug suddenly

definitions.definition: The branch of biology that deals with the study of drugs, their sources, properties, effects on living organisms and their therapeutic uses
A chemical substance that affects the structure and function of living organisms when introduced into the body
A substance produced by microorganisms that kills or inhibits the growth of other microorganisms
The excessive and harmful use of drugs that leads to physical and psychological dependence
A condition in which the body requires increasingly larger doses of a drug to achieve the same effect

importantQuestions.question: Define pharmacology and write any two sources of drugs.
importantQuestions.answer: Pharmacology is the branch of biology that deals with the study of drugs, their sources, properties, effects on living organisms and their therapeutic uses. Two sources: (1) Plants - digitalis from foxglove (2) Animals - insulin from pancreas

importantQuestions.question: What is drug abuse? Explain the harmful effects of drug abuse on human health.
importantQuestions.answer: Drug abuse is the excessive and harmful use of drugs that leads to physical and psychological dependence. Harmful effects: (1) Physical effects - damage to liver, heart, brain and other organs (2) Psychological effects - depression, anxiety, behavioral changes (3) Social effects - family problems, crime, loss of job (4) Development of tolerance requiring higher doses (5) Withdrawal symptoms when drug is stopped (6) Risk of infectious diseases like HIV/AIDS through shared needles (7) Economic burden on family and society

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a stimulant drug?

commonMistakes: Students often confuse drug tolerance with drug resistance
Writing incomplete definitions without mentioning 'effects on living organisms'
Forgetting to mention Alexander Fleming when discussing antibiotic discovery
Confusing stimulants with depressants - remember caffeine is stimulant, alcohol is depressant
Not writing sources of drugs with proper examples in answers
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 short question about drug sources or abuse in every BSEK paper
Always write the exact definition of pharmacology as it appears in textbook
Remember to give examples when listing drug sources
Drug abuse effects question appears frequently - memorize all physical, psychological and social effects
note_data.keyDiagram: Classification of drugs diagram showing main categories: Stimulants (caffeine, nicotine), Depressants (alcohol, morphine), Hallucinogens (LSD, marijuana), and Therapeutic drugs (antibiotics, vitamins). Also draw drug sources diagram showing Plants, Animals, Minerals, and Synthetic sources with examples.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define pharmacology and give two examples of drugs with their sources
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (5 marks) — required classification of drugs into antibiotics, analgesics and vaccines with one example each
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (3 marks) — tested definition of antibiotics and difference between broad-spectrum and narrow-spectrum antibiotics
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q6 (4 marks) — asked about sources of drugs (plants, animals, minerals) with specific examples

workedExample.question: Define pharmacology. Classify drugs on the basis of their action and give one example of each type. (5 marks)
steps: Write the exact textbook definition of pharmacology
State the classification: Antibiotics, Analgesics, Vaccines
Give one example for each class with proper spelling
Ensure examples match the drug classes correctly
workedExample.answer: Pharmacology is the branch of biology that deals with the study of drugs and their effects on living organisms. Classification: (1) Antibiotics - drugs that kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria, e.g., Penicillin (2) Analgesics - drugs that relieve pain, e.g., Aspirin (3) Vaccines - biological preparations that provide immunity against diseases, e.g., Polio vaccine
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct definition, 1 mark each for naming three classes, 1 mark each for correct examples (total 5 marks)

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for pharmacology definition.
Many students confuse analgesics with antibiotics. Remember analgesics treat pain while antibiotics treat bacterial infections only.
When giving examples of drugs, ensure correct spelling - write 'Penicillin' not 'Penicilin' and 'Aspirin' not 'Asprine' to avoid mark deduction.
note_data.quickRecap: Pharmacology studies drugs and their effects on living organisms. Drugs are classified as antibiotics (kill bacteria - Penicillin), analgesics (relieve pain - Aspirin), and vaccines (provide immunity - Polio vaccine). Sources include plants (Digitalis), animals (Insulin), and minerals (Calomel). Always write textbook-exact definitions for full marks.
